evidence: `src/domains/generation/types/generationRow.ts` exports `GenerationRow = GenerationRowDomain & Partial<GenerationRowLegacyAliases>`, so domain contracts and DB/API legacy aliases are merged into one widely shared type surface.

`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` combines normalization, logging, notification dispatch, callback invocation, rethrow helper, and notifier configuration re-exports, creating a broad cross-cutting entry point.

`src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx` colocates multiple device hooks plus a composite `useDeviceInfo` orchestration API in one high-import module, mixing low-level detection and high-level presentation-oriented derivations.

`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` mixes singleton acquisition with optional debug global mutation (`window.__supabase_client__`, `window.supabase`), blending production runtime boundary and diagnostics concerns.

findings.summary: Generation domain type is coupled to legacy transport aliases in one shared contract
evidence: `GenerationRow` is defined as `GenerationRowDomain & Partial<GenerationRowLegacyAliases>`, exposing `created_at`, `thumbnail_url`, and other boundary alias fields in the same type used as domain data.
`Shot.images` depends on `GenerationRowDomain[]`, but the module still publishes the merged `GenerationRow` type from the same location, encouraging consumers to depend on mixed-layer fields.
findings.suggestion: Keep `GenerationRowDomain` as the only domain export in this module, move alias fields to a dedicated mapper/DTO type file (e.g., `generationRowDto.ts`), and require explicit mapping at API/DB boundaries before data reaches domain-level consumers.

findings.summary: Error handling entrypoint owns too many responsibilities for one shared module
evidence: `handleError.ts` performs normalization, logging, toast/title/description construction, notification emission, callback dispatch, plus exports notifier configuration and a rethrow variant.
Feature hooks (`useAdjacentPagePreloader`, `useMediaGalleryItemState`) import this single facade directly, which makes unrelated subsystems converge on one broad utility contract.
findings.suggestion: Split the facade into narrower entry points (`normalizeAndLogError`, `notifyError`, `handleAndRethrow`) and keep notifier configuration in a dedicated bootstrap module, so consumers import only the behavior they need and ownership boundaries become explicit.

findings.summary: Mobile utilities module mixes primitive detection hooks and composite UI policy
evidence: `useMobile.tsx` defines `useIsMobile`, `useIsTablet`, `useIsTouchDevice`, and `useDeviceInfo` with derived UI policy (`mobileColumns`) in one module.
UI components consume only small slices (`useIsMobile`), but the shared module still owns broader orchestration concerns and multiple window listener setups.
findings.suggestion: Separate low-level device signal hooks into a `deviceSignals` module and move `useDeviceInfo`/`mobileColumns` policy into a higher-level `responsiveViewModel` hook so consumers opt into either primitive detection or composed UI policy explicitly.

findings.summary: Supabase client module couples runtime bootstrapping with debug global exposure
evidence: The same module that provides `getSupabaseClient()` singleton creation also exports `initializeSupabaseDebugGlobals()` that mutates `window` globals.
This blends integration runtime concerns and diagnostics wiring in one ownership boundary, even though debug setup is optional and environment-specific.
findings.suggestion: Move debug-global registration into a separate dev-only diagnostics module and keep `client.ts` focused on client lifecycle/bootstrap only; call diagnostics initialization from explicit development entrypoints.
